text: During early embryonic development of the nervous system-specific developmental genes need to be activated in a highly regulated and concerted manner. Genes need to be expressed (or silenced) at the right time and space. If this concerted regulation is disturbed this may lead to severe malformations in the later fetus. The type of malformation depends strongly on the timing of disturbance, i.e. during which developmental stage the disturbance happened. For the development of neural tube defects, especially neural tube closure, the disturbance must happen before neurulation at the stage when neuroepithelial cells arise. Therefore, for this AOP the imbalance of gene expression must occur before neural tube closure. However, compare AOP23 the inhibition of neural crest migration by HDAC inhibition happens later in embryonic development.
